title: Round 3 — the sequenced product, ready to triage
prompt: Round 3 · CONVERGE — your map became the product. Pool changes since round 2: Living shelf is gone as a standalone (merged into Wayfinder strip per your instruction — keeps now hang grabbable beneath the strip). Each of your four clusters was synthesized by meaning into one shippable concept, in your stated sequence: Open with anything (onboard) → Judge deck (review) → Wayfinder strip (navigate) → Sudden-death crowning (finalize — this one is the hybrid your gap note asked for, where brainstorming becomes elimination). The fifth option, The one-sitting journey, is all four beats as one end-to-end story. The gate is open: KEEP what ships, KILL what doesn't, MERGE what belongs together — or crown 🏁 a final if one of these IS the roadmap.

options.label: Judge deck
options.description: Flick-fast card triage that auto-deals a duel when two keeps are too close to call, building a live ranking as you go.

options.description: One strip along the studio's top edge IS the brainstorm — thumbnails narrow toward the winner, keeps hang grabbable beneath, and the right end is the glowing next-phase button.

options.label: The one-sitting journey
options.description: The whole product as one story — drop anything to open, flick-judge with a duel, steer by the wayfinder strip, and end crowned and shared, all in one sitting.

survey.elaborationPrompt: Triage time: which of these ship, in what order — and is the one-sitting journey the roadmap itself?
